claim: Each EntityType.Lifecycle shall be a Lifecycle value (the §Lifecycle keystone) with no parallel state machinery introduced.
why: The §Lifecycle keystone was introduced precisely so that every stateful aspect (Process, Entity, Goal, Operator) reuses one mechanism. Parallel state machinery would fracture the invariant surface and double the maintenance burden for each new aspect. In the Go port, the Lifecycle type is defined once in internal/ontology/lifecycle.go (State, Transition, Lifecycle structs); EntityType.Lifecycle embeds it directly (ontology.EntityType.Lifecycle is of type ontology.Lifecycle). The check check_entity_type_lifecycle_wellformed (internal/invariants/entity_checks.go) validates every EntityType.Lifecycle through the exact same lifecycleWellformedIssues BFS machinery that validates Process and Goal lifecycles -- no parallel state machinery. Wave 1 mechanical-honesty pass (2026-07-02) promoted this from STRUCTURAL to ENFORCED; in the Go port the shared-keystone reuse is directly visible in the check source.
